Rogers, Robert, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 23 June, 1656, B.A. 20 March, 1659, M.A. 1662, B.Med. 1666.
Pulman, Thomas, gent. New Inn Hall, matric. 23 June, 1656; B.A. from Exeter Coll. 28 Feb., 1659-60.
Gayer, Roger, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 1 April, 1656; B.A. from Magdalen Hall 1659, M.A. 1662.
Hillard, Thomas, gent. New Inn Hall, matric. 23 June, 1656.

Thorpe, John, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 15 June, 1657; chorister Magdalen Coll. 1659-60, B.A. 1 Feb., 1660-1; M.A. from Magdalen Hall 1663; one of these names rector of St. Michael Queenhithe, London, 1662; another vicar of Burford, Oxon, 1669, and father of John 1691, and of Jordan. See Bloxam, i. 83; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Pros(s)er, Thomas, 'ser.' New Inn Hall, matric. 29 Oct., 1657, B.A. 1661; vicar of Llansadwrn and Llanwrda, co. Carmarthen, 1663, and of Dorstone, co. Hereford, 1664, rector of Moccas, also co. Hereford, 1673; father of Thomas 1694. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Bullocke, William, 'ser.' New Inn Hall, matric. 15 June, 1657; B.A. 1661, one of these names vicar of Lilford, Northants, 1671, and of Little Oakley 1672. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Rownsevall, Samuel, cler. fil. New Inn Hall, matric. 15 June, 1657.

Scott, John, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 13 Dec., 1658, B. and D.D. 9 July, 1685 (s. Thomas, of Chippenham, Wilts, grazier), apprenticed in London for about 3 years; minister of St. Thomas in Southwark, rector of St. Peter-le-Poor 1678, lecturer of a church in Lombard Street, canon of St. Paul's 1685, rector of St. Giles-in-the-Fields 1691, until his death 10 March, 1694, buried in the church. See Ath. iv. 414; O.H.S. ix. 7; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Bickford, Arscott, pleb., 'sic mentitus est commensalis superior.' New Inn Hall, matric. 13 Dec., 1658, of Dunsland, Devon, son and heir of William, of Bickford Town, Devon, and St. Kevern, Cornwall, father of Edmund and William.

Mann, Thomas, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 21 March, 1658-9; possibly bar.-at-law, Inner Temple, 1667 (as of Yoxford, Suffolk, arm.); buried in the Temple church 2 Nov., 1669. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Cory, Samuel, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 21 March, 1658-9; B.A. 17 Jan., 1662-3, rector of Rattery, Devon, 1663, father of James and John 1683. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Clissold, Henry, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 21 March, 1658-9; B.A. from St. John's Coll. 1662, M.A. 1665.
Goddard, More (Goddart), pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 11 Dec., 1658, subs. "Mor" or "Ma."
Rogers, William, cler. fil. New Inn Hall, matric. 11 Dec., 1658; B.A. 21 Feb., 1660-1.
Eastway, John, cler. fil. New Inn Hall, matric. 13 Dec., 1658; B.A. 1662.
Stephens, Hugh (Steevens), "ser." New Inn Hall, matric. 11 Dec., 1658.
Lloyd, Thomas, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 21 March, 1658-9.
Lloyd, Lewis, "ser." New Inn Hall, matric. 11 Dec., 1658.
Miles, John (Myles), cler. fil. New Inn Hall, matric. 25 March, 1659-60.
Parry, Thomas, "ser." New Inn Hall, matric. 25 March, 1659, B.A. 1663.
Gwyn, George (Gwin), pleb. New Inn Hall. matric. 25 March, 1659.
Gwyn, John (Gwin), pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 25 March, 1659.

Rumsey, John, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 26 Oct., 1660; possibly bar.-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1669 (as of Sudbrook, co. Monmouth), and of the Inner Temple 1685; licenced 4 Jan., 1670-1, to marry Elizabeth Fisher, of St. Andrew, Holborn. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Bisco, Samuel, 'ministri fil.' New Inn Hall, matric. 6 June, 1660, one of these names vicar of Bisham, Berks, 1666, and of Wellingborough, Northants, 1690. See Marriage Licences, ed. Foster; & Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Birt, Walter (Byrt), pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 6 June, 1660, one of these names vicar of Ewerne Minster, Dorset, 1676. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Thomson, Jeremiah, cler. fil. New Inn Hall, matric. 6 June, 1660.
Herbert, William, gent. New Inn Hall, matric. 21 July, 1660.
Vertey, William, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 21 July, 1660.
Winter, Gregory, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 21 July, 1660.
Fisher, Samuel, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 6 June, 1660.
Harris, John, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 26 Oct., 1660.
Chaffey, William, New Inn Hall, matric. 21 July, 1660.

Avenant, Thomas, s. John, of King's Norton, co. Worcester, pleb., p.p. New Inn Hall, matric. 14 June, 1661, aged 18; rector of Avon Dassett, co. Warwick, 1676, vicar of Dormington, co. Hereford, 1685, and of Newent, co. Gloucester, 1691. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Rogers, Thomas, s. Robert, of Dean, Hants, minister. New Inn Hall, matric. 14 June, 1661, aged 15, B.A. 1664, M.A. 1667; perhaps vicar of Clun, Salop, 1671, canon of Hereford 1679, and rector of Shelve, Salop, 1686. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Tomkys, John, of Bilstone, co. Stafford, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 14 June, 1661, aged 18, B.A. 1664; M.A. from King's Coll., Cambridge, 1668; incorporated 13 July, 1669; vicar of Snitterfield, co. Warwick, 1682. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Middleton, Robert, s. Robert, of Taunton, Somerset, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 14 Nov., 1661, aged 18; M.A. by decree 5 July, 1671; perhaps vicar of Braintree, Essex, 1678, etc., and canon of Ely 1699. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Charman, Stephen, s. Stephen, of Hemsworth, co. York, minister. New Inn Hall, matric. 14 Nov., 1661, aged 18; B.A. 1 April, 1671, M.A. 5 July, 1671, rector of Liddiard Tregoze, Wilts, 1692. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Simcox, Josiah, s. John, of West Bromwich, co. Stafford, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 21 Feb., 1661-2, aged 17, B.A. 1665; vicar of Stratford-onAvon, co. Worcester, 1681. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Wetherell, John, s. St., of Askew, Yorks, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 16 July, 1661, aged 18, B.A. 1664, M.A. 1669; rector of Scruton, Yorks, 1670; father of John next-named. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Bellamy, Samuel, s. Richard, of Rampisham, Dorset, pleb., p.p. New Inn Hall, matric. 14 June, 1661, aged 20; one of these names vicar of Shalfleet, Isle of Wight, 1675. See Foster's Index.
Boughton, Nathaniel, s. John, of Batcombe, Dorset, minister. New Inn Hall, matric. 14 June, 1661, aged 20; one of these names vicar of Northover, Somerset, 1668, and of Ilchester 1672.
Kidley, William, s. William, of Andover, Hants, pleb. pp. New Inn Hall, matric. 24 May, 1661, aged 20; perhaps M.A. from Magdalen Coll., Cambridge, 1681. See Foster's Graduati Cantab.
Hancock, Nathaniel, s. Nicholas, of Shafstone, Dorset, pp. New Inn Hall, matric. 12 July, 1661, aged 16; perhaps vicar of Compton Chamberlaine, Wilts, 1693. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Pollard, John, s. Thomas, of St. Budeaux, Devon, p.p. New Inn Hall, matric. 12 July, 1661, aged 18, B.A. 1665; rector of Ashbury, Devon, 1669. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Parks, William, s. William, of Halesowen, Salop, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 12 July, 1661, aged 18; B.A. 9 March, 1664-5, as Parkes.
Williams, Owen, s. "William Thomas," of Beaumaris, co. Anglesea, p.p. New Inn Hall, matric. 12 July, 1661, aged 18.
Hughes, Henry (Hewse), s. Henry, of Hamill, co. Warwick, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 12 July, 1661, aged 18.
Reynell, Richard, s. William, of Torbryan, Devon, pleb. p.p. New Inn Hall, matric. 14 June, 1661, aged 20.
Harris, John, s. Giles, of Holsworthy, Devon, pleb. pp. New Inn Hall, matric. 14 June, 1661, aged 17.
Weare, John, s. William, of Kinton Magna, Devon, p.p. New Inn Hall, matric. 12 July, 1661, aged 19.
Crabb, John, s. Henry, of Chardstock, Dorset, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 14 June, 1661, aged 19.

Wagstaffe, Thomas, s. Th., of Stoke, co. Warwick, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 14 March, 1662-3, aged 19, B.A. 15 Oct., 1664, M.A. 20 June, 1667; born at Stafford 15 Feb., 1645, went to Pembroke Hall, Cambridge, 1659; chaplain to Earl of Denbigh and Sir Richard Temple, of Stowe, Bucks, rector of Martinthorpe alias Malthorpe, Rutland, 1669-91, curate of Stowe 1676, chancellor of Lichfield and canon 1684, deprived 1689, rector of St. Margaret Pattens and St. Gabriel, Fenchurch, London, 1685, until deprived in 1690 for not taking the oaths; licenced (V.G.) 4 Feb., 1686-7, to marry Martha Broughton, of St. Margaret's, Westminster, spinster; consecrated suffragan bishop of Norwich 23 Feb., 1693-4, afterwards practiced physic; died 17 Oct., 1712, buried at Bixley, co. Warwick, 20th; father of William 1700. See Ath. iv. 785; Foster's Index Eccl.; & Rawl. i. 51.

Trebeck, Andrew, cler. fil. New Inn Hall, subscribed 25 July, 1655, B.A. 29 March, 1658, M.A. 21 Jan., 1661-2, clerk Magdalen Coll. 1662-4, chaplain 1664-7; rector of Worcester St. Helen's 1668, and of St. Peter's 1674, father of the next-named. See Bloxam, ii. 72.

Cobden, William, s. Richard, of Midhurst, Sussex, pleb., p.p. New Inn Hall, matric. 19 July, 1662, aged 18; B.A. 9 Feb., 1665-6, M.A. 1668, rector of Ellesfield, Hants, 1668-1703, father of the last named and of the next named. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Hoskin, William, s. Rich, of Quethiock, Cornwall, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 17 Dec., 1662, aged 20; perhaps M.A. from New Coll. 18 Jan., 1664-5, as "Hoskins," and vicar of St. Mellion, Cornwall, 1674, as Hoskins. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Remphrey, Gilbert, s. Gilb., of St. Wem, Cornwall, p.p. New Inn Hall, matric. 14 March, 1662-3, aged 18, B.A. 1666; vicar of Morthoe 1697, and of Brendon, Devon, 1699; father of the next-named. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Shutt, William, s. William, of Bayford, Somerset, p.p. New Inn Hall., matric. 14 March, 1662-3, aged 18, B.A. 1666, M.A. 1669 (as Shute); perhaps rector of Holwell, Dorset, 1678. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Ivie, Hugh, s. Hugh, of Bruton, Somerset, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 17 Dec., 1662, aged 18; B.A. 1666, M.A. 1669, rector of Foxcot and of Writhlington, Somerset, 1669. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Rogers, John, s. Rob., of Wappenham, Northants, minister. New Inn Hall, matric. 14 March, 1662-3, aged 17; B.A. 15 Oct., 1664, M.A. 20 June, 1667. See London Marriage Licences, ed. Foster.
Pincombe, John, s. William, of Filleigh, Devon, pleb., p.p. New Inn Hall, matric. 15 May, 1662, aged 16, B.A. 1665.
Grove, Daniel, s. Henry, of Ludley, co. Worcester, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 19 July, 1662, aged 18; B.A. 1666.
Jones, Humphrey, s. Evan, of Overton Maddock, co. Flint, minr. pp. New Inn Hall, matric. 19 July, 1662, aged 21.
Carter, Joseph, s. John, of Kings Norton, co. Worcester, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 15 May, 1662, aged 23.
Herring, John, s. John, of Hill Bishops, pleb. pp. New Inn Hall, matric. 15 May, 1662, aged 18.

Perkins, William, s. Henr., of Hanley, Dorset, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 17 July, 1663, aged 18; B.A. 1667, M.A. 15 March, 1669-70; one of these names vicar of Ormesby, Yorks, 1676, and rector of Straxton, co. Lincoln, 1686. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Havard, William (Haverde) s. Walter, of Wimborne, Dorset, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 11 Dec., 1663, aged 17; B.A. 1667, M.A. 1670, as Havard.
Orchard, William, s. Rob., of Little Windsor, Dorset, pleb. p.p. New Inn Hall, matric. 27 March, 1663, aged 17.
Pearse, William, s. William, of Chardstock, Dorset, p.p. New Inn Hall, matric. 23 May, 1663, aged 18.
Poynter, Samuel, s. Sam., of Oxford (city), minister. New Inn Hall, matric. 22 July, 1663, aged 18.
Beale, Daniel, s. William, of co. Gloucester, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 27 March, 1663, aged 16.
Butler, John, s. John, of Hungerford, Berks, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 30 Oct., 1663, aged 19.

Wheeler, Maurice, s. Maur., of St. Giles, Dorset, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 1 April, 1664, aged 16, B.A. 1667; chaplain or minor canon Christ Church, M.A. 1670; rector of St. Ebbes, Oxford, master of the collegiate school in Gloucester; rector of Sibbertoft 1680-4, of Wappenham 1712-15, and of Thorpe Mandeville, (all) Northants, 1720, canon of Lincoln 1708; buried at Wappenham 7 or 9 Oct., 1727. See Ath. iv. 785; Fasti, ii. 319; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Garbrand, John, s. "Tobias,", of Abingdon, Berks, doctoris. New Inn Hall, matric. 16 July, 1664, aged 17; B.A. 28 Jan., 1667-8, bar.-at-law, Inner Temple, 1673 (alias Herkes). See Ath. iv. 786; Foster's Judges and Barristers; & D.N.B.

Antram, John, s. Joh., of Hilton, Dorset, cler. New Inn Hall. matric. 17 March, 1664-5, aged 20; B.A. from New Coll. 1668, M.A. from New Inn Hall 1678, vicar of Hilton, Dorset, 1669. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Kettilby, Thomas, s. Conisby, of Henly, Salop, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 1 April, 1664, aged 18; B.A. 1667, rector of Greet, Salop, 1669, vicar of Avenbury, co. Hereford, 1678. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Pearse, Nathan, s. Nath., of Membury, Devon, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 17 March, 1664-5, aged 16; vicar of Isle Abbots, Somerset, 1671, rector of Musbery, Devon, 1672. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Francke, Abraham, s. William, of Poughill, Devon, minister. New Inn Hall, matric. 6 May, 1664, aged 18; rector of Poughill 1672-1713; father of William 1702. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Abbott, Francis, s. Francis, of Wimborne, Dorset, pleb., p.p. New Inn Hall, matric. 1 April, 1664, aged 17.
Cockram, John, s. Bruin, of Swanage, Dorset, minister. New Inn Hall, matric. 1 April, 1664, aged 18.
Stackhouse, John, s. Roger, of London, gent. New Inn Hall, matric. 25 May, 1664, aged 15.

Webber, John, s. Sim., of Broadwood Kelly, Devon, minister. New Inn Hall, matric. 28 April, 1665, aged 18; rector of Broadwood Kelly 1672; brother of Henry 1672, father of Simon 1692. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Brooker, Henry, s. Joh., of Sulham, Berks, minister. New Inn Hall, matric. 13 Feb., 1665-6, aged 15; B.A. 1669, M.A. 1672.
Franklin, William, s. William, of Lyddiard, Wilts, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 28 April, 1665, aged 15; B.A. 1669.
Deane, Freeman, s. Charles, of Hinton Martell, Dorset, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 10 Nov., 1665, aged 16.
Paddon, John, s. Joh., of Winkley, Devon, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 28 April, 1665, aged 18.
Pym, Charles, s. Tho., of Bristol, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 7 July, 1665, aged 15.

Buckingham, Samuel, s. Robert, of Crediton, Devon, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 30 March, 1666, aged 19; B.A. from Hart Hall 1669, vicar of Studham, Beds, 1672, and of Worksop, Notts, 1674, licenced 11 Feb., 1674-5, to marry Mary Vaux, of Whipsnead, Beds, spr. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus & Marriage Licences, ed. Foster.

Conyers, (Sir) John, s. Christopher, of Horden, co. Durham, bart. New Inn Hall, matric. 14 Dec., 1666, aged 17; 3rd baronet, died 14 Sept., 1719, buried in Great Stoughton church, Hunts. [Addenda: licenced (v.g.) 16 Jan., 1680-1, to marry Mary Lee, of Farley, in East Grinstead, Sussex, spinster, aged 17.]

Hawkins, John s. Henry, of Creed, Cornwall, pleb. pp. New Inn Hall, matric. 30 May, 1666, aged 19; B.A. from Exeter Coll. 5 July, 1670; incorporated at Cambridge 1677, and M.A. Pembroke Coll., Cambridge, 1677, vicar of St. Ewney-juxta-Lalant, Cornwall, 1676; brother of Titus 1669. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Ven, Denis, s. Richard Fenn, of Otterton, Devon, p.p. New Inn Hall, matric. 25 May, 1666, aged 18, as Fenn; B.A. from Exeter Coll. 18 Jan., 1669-70 (as Ven); rector of Dodbrooke 1671, and vicar of Holbeton, Devon, 1673. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Wood, William, s. William, of St. Issey, Cornwall, p.p. New Inn Hall, matric. 30 March, 1666, aged 16; vicar of Colan Parva 1677, and of Withiel, (both) Cornwall, 1681; perhaps father of William 1696. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Jones, Michael, s. "Joh. Roberts", of Llanunda, co. Carnarv., pp. New Inn Hall, matric. 14 Dec., 1666, aged 18; one of these names vicar of Eastyn (or Queenhope, Flints), 1686. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Cobden, Richard, s. John, of West Deane, Sussex, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 14 Dec., 1666, aged 17; B.A. 1670, M.A. 1673, rector of Wood Eaton, Oxon, 1674. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Tutte, John, s. Randolph, of Chichester, gent. New Inn Hall, matric. 7 April, 1666, aged 20, B.C.L. 1673; vicar of Arundel 1674, and rector of Selsey, Sussex, 1677. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Haysome, Robert, s. Rob., of Anderson, Dorset, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 6 April, 1666, aged 20; B.A. 1669, M.A. 1672, rector of Little Langford, Wilts, 1674. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Hart, Robert, s. John, of Colyton Rawleigh, Devon, pleb. pp. New Inn Hall, matric. 30 May, 1666, aged 18; vicar of Colyton Rawleigh 1670. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Walker, Edward, s. Richard, of Cleobury, Salop, p.p. New Inn Hall, matric. 6 April, 1666, aged 18, B.C.L. 12 May, 1694.
Osborne, Samuel, s. Joh., of West Woodhay, Berks, minister. New Inn Hall, matric. 31 May, 1666, aged 15; B.A. 1670.
Osborne, John, s. Joh., of Bampton, Oxon, minister. New Inn Hall, matric. 13 July, 1666, aged 18; "a nonjuror."
Comyn, John (Comin), s. William, of Blewbury, Berks, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 28 March, 1666, aged 18.
Francklyn, Thomas, s. Rich., of Wroughton, Wilts, gent. New Inn Hall, matric. 18 Jan., 1666-7, aged 18.
Fenn, Dennis, s. Rich., of Otterton, Devon, pp. New Inn Hall, matric. 25 May, 1666, aged 18.
Fenton, Bruce, s. Joh., of Bloxham, Oxon, pp. New Inn Hall, matric. 30 March, 1666, aged 19.
Bezar, Thomas, s. Gasp. of Kington, co. Hereford, p.p. New Inn Hall, matric. 13 July, 1666.

Bradford, William, s. Andrew, of Otterton, Devon, pleb., p.p. New Inn Hall, matric. 6 March, 1667-8, aged 20; B.A. from Exeter Coll. 1671, rector of Turner's Puddle, Dorset, 1680. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Pottle, William, s. William, of Wimbourne, Dorset, p.p. New Inn Hall, matric. 17 May, 1667, aged 18; student of Middle Temple 1676; buried in the Temple church 21 Aug., 1699. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Butt, Robert, s. R., of Bradford Abbas, Dorset, minister. New Inn Hall, matric. 30 April, 1667, aged 17; rector of Affpuddle, Dorset, 1685. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Dynham, John (Dinham), s. Tho., of Launceston, Cornwall, pleb., p.p. New Inn Hall, matric. 29 Nov., 1667, aged 16; B.A. from Exeter Coll. 1671, as Denham.
Pinsent, Ellis, s. Edw., of Lodgewell (Loddiswell). Devon, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 17 May, 1667, aged 17; B.A. 8 Feb., 1670-1.
Butter, James, s. Joh., of Colyton, Devon, pleb., p.p. New Inn Hall, matric. 29 March, 1667, aged 18; father of the next named.
Bellinger, 'Deverox,' s. Edw., of Aston Tyrrold, Berks, minister. New Inn Hall, matric. 29 March, 1667, aged 18.
Quarterman, Samuel, s. Dan., of Windsor, pleb. p.p. New Inn Hall, matric. 6 Dec., 1667, aged 17; B.A. 1671.
Willy, John, s. Tho., of South Petherton, Somerset, pleb. p.p. New Inn Hall, matric. 8 Nov., 1667, aged 18.
Jones, Owen, s. Owen, of Bryncross, co. Carnarv. pp. New Inn Hall, matric. 29 March, 1667, aged 15.
Horsington, Jasper, s. Giles, of Westminster, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 26 April, 1667, aged 18.
Fidkin, Philip, pp. New Inn Hall, matric. 23 May, 1667, B.A. 23 March, 1670-71.

Acland, John (Ackland), s. J., of Branton, Devon, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 18 March, 1668-9, aged 16; B.A. 1672, M.A. 1675, incorp. at Cambridge 1680, rector of Bittadon, Devon, 1681, preb. of Priors Bodmin in church of Endelion, Cornwall, 1682, rector of Littleham, Devon, 1682. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Keepe, Henry, s. Charles, of London, gent. New Inn Hall, matric. 17 July, 1668, aged 16; student of Inner Temple 1668, wrote "Monumenta Westmonasteriensia," etc.; died in Carter Lane, near St. Paul's cathedral, May, 1688; buried in the church of St. Gregory. See Ath. iv. 238; & Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Wooddeson, Samuel, s. John, of Windsor, Berks, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 12 Feb., 1668-9, aged 18; chorister of Magdalen Coll. 1662-5, B.A. from St. John's Coll. 29 Feb., 1671-2; rector of Huntspill, Somerset, 1679; brother of Richard 1670. See Bloxam, i. 93; & Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Tooker, Nicholas, s. Edm., of Lamerton, Devon, p.p. New Inn Hall, matric. 18 March, 1668-9, aged 20 (? 22); B.A. from Lincoln Coll. 1673; perhaps vicar of Virginstow, 1669-84, and of Hittisleigh, (both) Devon, 1682-1716, as Tucker. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Fry, Stephen, s. Joh., of Burseys, Dorset, gent. New Inn Hall, matric. 11 Dec., 1668, aged 18; B.A. from Trinity Coll. 1672, M.A. 1675, B.Med. 1682, D.Med. 1685; died 24 March, 1710; will at Oxford proved 21 Aug., 1710. See Hearne, ii. 77, 365.

Pitterd, Robert, s. Christopher, of Martock, Somerset, p.p. New Inn Hall, matric. 7 May, 1668, aged 18; vicar of Hazlebury Plucknett, Somerset, 1674. and rector of Thorne Coffin, Somerset, 1690. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Eare, Robert, s. Richard, of S. Breock, Cornwall, pp. New Inn Hall, matric. 20 Nov., 1668, aged 17; rector of St. John's, Cornwall, 1681. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Butler, Henry, s. H., of Handley, Dorset, arm. New Inn Hall, matric. 18 March, 1668-9, aged 18; of Handley, M.P. Shaftesbury 1685-7, then styled a knight.
Taylor, William, s. "Dig.," of St. Breock, Cornwall, minister. New Inn Hall, matric. 5 May, 1668, aged 19, B.A. 29 Feb., 1671-2.
Cherrett, William, s. Tho., of Corf Mullen, Dorset, p.p. New Inn Hall, matric. 20 Nov., 1668, aged 16; B.A. 1672.
Keene, Philip, s. William, of Dorchester, Oxon, pp. New Inn Hall, matric. 18 March, 1668-9, aged 18, B.A. 1672.
Raymond, Edward, s. Tho., of Hawkchurch, Dorset, p.p. New Inn Hall, matric. 11 Dec., 1668, aged 22; B.A. 1672.
Rugg, Jacob (James in Mat. Reg.), p.p. New Inn Hall, matric. 28 July, 1668.

Knight, John, s. William, of Staplegrove, Somerset, pp. New Inn Hall, matric. 2 April, 1669, aged 17; B.A. 1673, M.A. 1675 (incorporated at Cambridge 1676), B. and D.D. 1698, vicar of Banbury, Oxon, rector of Tusmore 1683, and of Broughton, Oxon, 1693, until his death 10 Sept., 1712, aged 61; buried there. See Fasti, ii. 348; & Rawl. iii. 270.

Hind, Charles, s. Lewis, of Oxford, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 10 Dec., 1669, aged 15; B.A. 1673, M.A. 1676, (called "Hine" in Mat. Reg.), perhaps rector of Waterstock, Oxon, 1677; father of Charles 1704, and of Edward 1714, brother of Edward 1673.

Head, Edward, s. John, of Peasmore, Berks, minister. New Inn Hall, matric. 2 April, 1669, aged 17; B.A. 1672, M.A. 1675, rector of Peasmore 1678-80, and perhaps vicar of Eisey 1683, and of Latton, Wilts, 1710. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Squire, Samuel, s. Rich., of Blackford (Somerset), pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 2 April, 1669, aged 18; rector of Durnford, Wilts, 1673; brother of Daniel and Richard, and father of the next, and of Francis. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Mallett, Thomas, s. Tho., of Honyborne, co. Gloucester, pp. New Inn Hall, matric. 1 July, 1669, aged 17; B.A. from Merton Coll. 1673, M.A. 5 Feb., 1675-6, rector of Whitney, co. Hereford, 1677. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Poulton, Michael, s. Joh., of Ichilhampton, Wilts, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 24 March, 1669-70, aged 16; B.A. 1673, M.A. 1676, canon of Lincoln 1687, rector of Lowick, Northants, 1693. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Flexman, James, s. I., of Torrington, Devon, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 18 March, 1669-70, aged 17; B.A. from Exeter Coll. 1673, rector of Wear Gifford, Devon, 1686. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Cole, John, s. Fr., of Buckland, Dorset, p.p. New Inn Hall, matric. 19 Nov., 1669, aged 19; one of these names rector of West Chickerel, Dorset, 1682. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Loe, George, s. G., of Hales Owen, co. Worcester, pp. New Inn Hall, matric. 18 March, 1669-70, aged 17; B.A. 1673, rector of Claverton, Somerset, 1688. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Moore, Thomas, s. Dan., of Yealmpton, Devon, p.p. New Inn Hall, matric. 21 May, 1669, aged 22; perhaps rector of Dodbrooke, Devon, 1674. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Cory, Richard, s. R(ichard), of St. Wenn, Cornwall, minister. New Inn Hall, matric. 19 Nov., 1669, aged 19.
Nicholas, Philip, s. R., of All Cannings, Wilts, gent. New Inn Hall, matric. 24 March, 1669-70, aged 15.
Blake, Ferdinando, s. Ferdinando, of Brixton, Devon, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 21 May, 1669, aged 18.
Berriman, John, s. George, of Bideford, Devon, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 2 April, 1669, aged 18.
Royall, Henry, s. William, of Bowdon, Somerset, p.p. New Inn Hall, matric. 1 July, 1669, aged 18.
Harbin, John, s. Joh., of West Milton, Dorset, pp. New Inn Hall, matric. 16 July, 1669, aged 18.
Tapp, Roger, s. Joh., of North Molton, Devon, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 1 July, 1669, aged 18.
Snowe, John, s. Tho., of Bulford, Wilts, p.p. New Inn Hall, matric. 18 March, 1669-70, aged 17.

Constantine, John, y.s. William, of Merly, Dorset, equitis. New Inn Hall, matric. 14 Jan., 1670-1, aged 17; of the Middle Temple 1669, perhaps beneficed in Somersetshire 1688-1724, brother of Henry 1658. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg. & Index Ecclesiasticus.

Brice, Henry, s. Steph., of Witney, Oxon, gent. New Inn Hall, matric. 19 May, 1670, aged 15; demy Magdalen Coll. 1671-8, B.A. 13 Feb., 1673-4, M.A. 1676, fellow 1678-83, rector of Braudeston 1681, until his death in 1692. See Bloxam, v. 318.

Merry, John, s. Th., of Milton Abbas, Dorset, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 8 July, 1670, aged 20; B.A. 1674, vicar of Stourpaine, Dorset, 1677, rector of Steepleton Iwerne, Dorset, 1691. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Symonds, Thomas, s. Joh., of Corfe, Dorset, p.p. New Inn Hall, matric. 19 May, 1670, aged 19; B.A. from Magdalen Hall 26 Feb., 1673-4; perhaps vicar of East Lulworth, Dorset, 1674. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Ansty, Thomas (Aynstee), s. Tho., of Wimborne, Dorset, cler. New Inn Hall, matric. 4 March, 1670-1, aged 15; B.A. 1674, M.A. 1677, vicar of Sopley, Hants, 1689. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Roberts, Edward, s. Ed., of Llanarmon, co. Denbigh, p.p. New Inn Hall, matric. 21 Oct., 1670, aged 18; one of these names rector of Llandegla, co. Denbigh, 1685. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Wall, Joseph, s. Rich., of Maltock, Somerset, minister. New Inn Hall, matric. 4 March, 1670-1, aged 16; vicar of Milton Abbas and Lilborne, Dorset, 1675-1721. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Breamer, John (Bremer), s. Fran., of Northstreete, Berks, p.p. New Inn Hall, matric. 29 April, 1670, aged 16; B.A. from Corpus Christi Coll. 1 Feb., 1675-6.
Goodyer, Edward (Gooddiar), s. Franc., of Courteenhall, Northants, minister. New Inn Hall, matric. 17 May, 1670, aged 16; B.A. from All Souls' Coll. 1674.
Gillingham, Thomas, s. Th., of Honybrooke, Dorset, gent. New Inn Hall, matric. 17 May, 1670, aged 16; B.A. 20 Jan., 1673-4, M.A. 1677.
Eaton, Thomas, s. Richard, of Ipplepen, Devon, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 17 May, 1670, aged 17.

Sheldon, Michael, s. Henry, of Boninghall, Salop, p.p. New Inn Hall, matric. 14 July, 1671, aged 18, B.A. 1675, M.A. 1679; vicar of South Hill, Beds, 1682; licenced (V.G.) 7 March, 1681-2, to marry Susanna Colbrand, of St. Giles-in-theFields, Middlesex, spinster. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Cuffe, William, s. Jerem., of Sherbourne, Dorset, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 14 April, 1671, aged 17; B.A. 1674, M.A. 1677, perhaps rector of Axbridge, Somerset, 1679-82, and canon of Wells 1679, brother of Jeremiah 1667. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus & Baker's Northants, ii. 257.

Terry, Peter, s. James, of Odiham, Hants, minister. New Inn Hall, matric. 27 May, 1671, aged 17, B.A. 19 Jan., 1674-5, M.A. 1677; rector of Salisbury St. Martin 1689, canon 1694-1727, and precentor of Sarum 1703, rector of Upper Clatford, Hants, 1709. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Holt, Thomas, s. Tho., of Petersfield, Hants, gent. New Inn Hall, matric. 27 May, 1671, aged 15; perhaps demy Magdalen Coll., 1674-81, B.A. 1675, M.A. 1678, incorporated at Cambridge 1680; buried 21 Dec., 1684; brother of John 1675, but see next entry and Bloxam, vi. 2.

Harding, George, s. Joh., of Kisworth, Dorset, gent. New Inn Hall, matric. 20 Oct., 1671, aged 16; scholar Wadham Coll. 1672, B.A. 1675, fellow 1677-84, M.A. 1678, rector of Ardley, Oxon, 1684, of Strixton, Northants, 1688. See Gardiner, 294; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Peche, John, s. William, of Chichester, Sussex, gent. New Inn Hall, matric. 22 March, 1671-2, aged 16; B.A. 1675, M.A. 1678, admitted a licentiate of the college of physicians 1684, as Pechey. See Ath. iv. 787; & Munk's Roll, i. 433.

Gillingham, Richard, s. Rich., of Bishops Caundle, Dorset, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 27 May, 1671, aged 17; B.A. 11 Feb., 1674-5, M.A. 1685, vicar of Martock, Somerset, 1684, rector of Ham, Wilts, 1688. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Kenrick, Christian, s. Sam., of Leigh, co. Glouc., pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 14 July, 1671, aged 16; B.A. 1679 (as "Kendrick"); vicar of Eckington, co. Worcester, 1681, also as Kendrick. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Farbrother, Roger, s. Joh., of West Haughton, co. Lancaster, p.p. New Inn Hall, matric. 22 March, 1671-2, aged 20; B.A. 1675, vicar of Holyrood, Southampton, 1682. See Rawl. v. 374; & Foster's Index Eccl.
Goldesbrough, Nicholas, s. Augustine, of East Knoyle, Wilts, pp. New Inn Hall, matric. 18 March, 1671-2, aged 21; B.A. 1680, rector of East Stoke, Dorset, 1685. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Webber, Henry, s. Sim., of Broadwoodkelly, Devon, minister. New Inn Hall, matric. 22 March, 1671-2, aged 17; brother of John 1665.
Stuckey, John, s. William, of Merriott, Somerset, p.p. New Inn Hall, matric. 14 July, 1671, aged 17, B.A. 4 Feb., 1675-6.
Pretherch, Thomas (Prydderih), s. Th., of Llandewycombe, co. Brecon, p.p. New Inn Hall, matric. 14 April, 1671, aged 17.
Howse, Job (Howes), s. Rob., of Stratford-on-Avon, co. Warwick (pleb. ?). New Inn Hall, matric. 2 June, 1671, aged 17.
Jones, William, s. Aaron, of Wantage, Berks, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 22 March, 1671-2; aged 18. See Ath. iv. 787.
Taylor, Thomas, s. Th., of Lynn Regis, Dorset, gent. New Inn Hall, matric. 8 April, 1671, aged 17, B.A. 1674.
Glanvill, Samuel, s. Tho., of Kingsteynton, Devon, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 7 April, 1671, aged 17.
Hall, William, s. Tho., of Tunworth, Hants, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 15 April, 1671, aged 17.

Courtis, Oliver (Courtice), s. O., of Broadwood, Devon, p.p. New Inn Hall, matric. 21 March, 1672-3, aged 18; B.A. from Trinity Coll. 1676, vicar of Sidmouth, Devon, 1682, father of the last named. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Prichard, Evan, s. Rich., of Cerist, co. Merioneth, p.p. New Inn Hall, matric. 21 March, 1672-3, aged 19; rector of Garthbeibio 1685, and of Llangadfan, co. Montgomery, 1705. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Toup, Onesiphorus, s. Onesiph., of Maiden Newton, Dorset, minister. New Inn Hall, matric. 9 July, 1672, aged 18; rector of Bridport, Dorset, 1683; father of Jonathan 1703. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Still, Samuel, p.p. New Inn Hall, matric. 17 May, 1672, B.A. 29 Jan., 1675-6; rector of Brockley 1686, and of Wraxall, Somerset, 1694. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Durston, Richard (Durson), s. Tho., of Axbridge, Somerset, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 29 Nov., 1672, aged 19; B.A. 1676, as Durston.
Kearsley, James, s. "Noachi,", of West Houghton, co. Lancaster, pp. New Inn Hall, matric. 9 May, 1672, aged 18; B.A. 22 Feb., 1676-7.
Coleman, William, s. Th., of Chardstock, Dorset, p.p. New Inn Hall, matric. 21 March 1672-3, aged 18; B.A. from Pembroke Coll. 1676.
Knight, William, s. Ric., of Stoke Priors, co. Worcester, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 10 July, 1672, aged 18; father of Henry 1711.
Cart, Joseph, s. William, of Aldchurch, co. Worcester, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 23 May, 1672, aged 18; B.A. 29 Jan., 1675-6.
Stone, Azariah, s. Clifton, of Hope Says, Salop, minister. New Inn Hall, matric. 3 Feb., 1672-3, aged 17, B.A. 1676, M.A. 1679.
Smith, Humphrey, s. Joh., of Badgeworth, Somerset, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 7 March, 1672-3, aged 17, B.A. 1676.
Crump, Benjamin, s. James, of Hangor, par. Bromley, Wilts, minister. New Inn Hall, matric. 26 July, 1672, aged 19.
Head, John, s. Tho., of Langley, Berks, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 9 May, 1672, aged 16; father of Richard 1706.
Welch, Stephen, s. William, of Chardstock, Dorset, p.p. New Inn Hall, matric. 21 March, 1672-3, aged 18.
Wheeler, John, s. Joh., of King's Cleere, Hants, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 14 March, 1672-3, aged 18.
Kempe, Ralph, s. Ralph, of Winchcombe, co. Gloucr., pp. New Inn Hall, matric. 21 June, 1672, aged 17.
Moyse, John (Moyce), s. Joh., of Benenden, Kent, pp. New Inn Hall, matric. 28 March, 1672; aged 20.
Raymond, John, s. Tho., of Hawkchurch, Dorset, p.p. New Inn Hall, matric. 9 July, 1672, aged 19.

Ancketyll, Edward, (fil. Edward), of Wraxhall, Somerset, cler. New Inn Hall, matric. 12 Dec., 1673, aged 17; B.A. 1677, M.A. 1680, rector of Clapton, Somerset, 1686, until his death in 1698. See Weaver, 254, 303.
Henvill, Matthias, s. Ralph, of Milverton, Somerset, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 2 May, 1673, aged 21; B.A. 3 March, 1680-1, M.A. 1681.
Crofts, John, s. Jos., of Mottistone, Isle of Wight, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 31 Oct., 1673, aged 18.
Hubert, Daniel, s. Fr., of Witney, Oxon, pp. New Inn Hall, matric. 5 Dec., 1673, aged 17; B.A. 1677.
Kelson, Nicholas, s. N., of Theale, Somerset, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 27 June, 1673, aged 18.
Boone, Thomas, s. Hug., of Paulet, Somerset, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 27 June, 1673, aged 16.
Trym, John, s. H., of Holdsworthy, Devon, pp. New Inn Hall, matric. 13 June, 1673, aged 20.

Gillingham, Robert, s. Nic., of Lydlinch, Dorset, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 12 March, 1674-5, aged 14; B.A. 1678, M.A. 1681, licenced (v.g.) 9 June, 1685, to marry Cecilia Brome, of Curry Mallet, Somerset, vicar of Isle Brewers, Somerset, 1685, and Isle Abbotts 1686, rector of Eastham, Somerset, 1692. See Foster's Index Eccl.

North, John, s. John, of Chilton, Berks, p.p. New Inn Hall, matric. 14 July, 1674, aged 18; B.A. 1679, M.A. 1682, rector of Laughton, Sussex, 1685, vicar of Great Missenden, Bucks, 1709. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Radford, William, s. William, of Ubley, Somerset, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 9 July, 1674, aged 22, B.A. 1678; rector of Marksbury, Somerset, 1682; father of the next. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Ireland, John, s. William, of Kingswood, Wilts, pp. New Inn Hall, matric. 3 April, 1674, aged 17; B.A. 1677, rector of Laverton, Somerset, 1682. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Berry, Bartholomew, s. Thomas, of Swimbridge, Devon, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 7 April, 1674, aged 18; B.A. 1677 (as Bury).
Frost, John, s. William, of Crediton, Devon, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 3 April, 1674, aged 18; B.A. 21 Feb., 1677-8.
Thomas, David, s. Rees, of Cowbridge, co. Glamorgan, p.p. New Inn Hall, matric. 10 April, 1674, aged 20.
Thomas, John, s. Tho., of Trawsfynydd, co. Merioneth, p.p. New Inn Hall, matric. 6 April, 1674, aged 19.
Sage, Elisha, s. Geo., of Bishops Nympton, Devon, p.p. New Inn Hall, matric. 7 April, 1674, aged 18.

Cornish, Henry, s. H., of Stanton Harcourt, Oxon, minister. New Inn Hall, matric. 9 March, 1675-6, aged 15; B.A. from Lincoln Coll. 1679, M.A. 1682, a commissioner of stamps, M.P. Shaftesbury 1698 till expelled 13 Feb., 1699, died 25 May, 1725, aged 63. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.

Morris, Claver, s. William, of Manstone, Dorset, minister. New Inn Hall matric. 13 March, 1675-6, aged 16; B.A. 1679, M.A. 1682, B. Med. 1685, D.Med. 1691, an extra licentiate of the college of physicians 1683; died at Wells, Somerset, 19 March, 1726-7, M.I. See Munk's Roll, i. 431.

Hinton, Thomas, s. Laurence, of Chilbolton, Hants, cler. New Inn Hall, matric. 26 March, 1675, aged 17; B.A. 1678, M.A. 1681, perhaps vicar of Addington, Surrey, 1710, rector of Lasham, Hants, 1713; brother of Laurence 1665, and of John 1671. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Jones, John, s. J., of Llansillian, co. Denbigh, pp. New Inn Hall, matric. 6 July, 1675, aged 20; B.A. from Trinity Coll. 1 Dec. 1681, usher of the free school at St. Albans, Herts; died 1686; buried in the large church of St. Alban. See Ath. iv. 201.

Savage, John, s. J., of Bloxworth, Dorset, cler. New Inn Hall, matric. 26 March, 1675, aged 16; B.A. 1678, M.A. 1681; rector of Turners Puddle, Dorset, 1686-1723, and of Northborough, Northants, 1696-1723. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Macham, Elias, s. Nath., of Derby (town), cler. New Inn Hall, matric. 20 May, 1675, aged 18; rector of Stanninghall 1700, and of Colby, Norfolk, 1703; his father vicar of Derby St. Werburgh 1662-85. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Turner, Charles, s. Joh., of Treheneglys (Trehanvis), Cornwall, gent. (subscribes "cler."). New Inn Hall, matric. 17 May, 1675, aged 17; vicar of Davidstow, Cornwall, 1683; father of Charles 1708. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Randolph, John, s. Tho., of Lighthorne, co. Warwick, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 28 Feb., 1675-6, aged 17, B.A. 1679; M.A. from St. Alban Hall 1682, vicar of Willoughby, co. Warwick, 1682. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Ford, John, s. Joh., of " Nightinburg," Sussex, cler. New Inn Hall, matric. 16 Oct., 1675, aged 17; B.A. 1679, M.A. 1682, perhaps vicar of Cutcombe, Somerset, 1699. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Adams, Francis (Addams), s. Robert, of Houstone, Sussex, gent. New Inn Hall, matric. 9 March, 1675-6, aged 16; B.A. 1679.
Holt, John, s. Th., of Petersfield, Hants, cler. New Inn Hall, matric. 14 May, 1675, aged 16; brother of Thomas 1671.
Jones, Andrew, s. Griffin, of Caerinion, co. Montgom. pp. New Inn Hall, matric. 26 March, 1675, aged 19; B.A. 1678.
Pougsley, John, s. James, of Kilkhampton, Cornwall, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 18 June, 1675, aged 18.
Lowman, Fortescue, s. F., of Cadeleigh, Devon, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 2 March, 1675-6, aged 18.
Parmynter, John, s. Nic., of Ilfracombe, Devon, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 14 May, 1675, aged 18.
Oldham, Luke (Oldom), s. L., of Derby (town), p.p. New Inn Hall, matric. 20 May, 1675, aged 16.

Johnson, Christopher, s. Joh., of Churchham, co. Gloucester, pp. New Inn Hall, matric. 8 July, 1676, aged 18; B.A. from Queen's Coll. 1680; incorporated at Cambridge 1691, and M.A. from King's Coll. 1691 (incorporated at Oxford 9 July, 1692), D.D. (per Literas Reg.) 1701; brother of Thomas 1669.
Gold, Anthony, s. Sylvester, of Oxford, pp. New Inn Hall, matric. 17 Nov., 1676, aged 16; B.A. 1680, M.A. 1683.
Davies, Richard, s. J., of Bettus, co. Denbigh, paup. New Inn Hall, matric. 26 June, 1676, aged 19.

French, John, s. Th., of Shute, Devon, pp. New Inn Hall, matric. 6 April, 1677, aged 19; B.A. from Exeter Coll. 1680; M.A. from Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge, 1699, rector of Seaborough, Somerset, 1684, vicar of Ilminster 1685, rector of Earnshill 1694, of Cricket Malherbe, Somerset, 1699. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Whitehall, Robert, s. Rich., of Stanton Harcourt, Oxon, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. entry 14 March, 1677-8, aged 14, postmaster Merton Coll. 1679, B.A. 1682, M.A. 1684, fellow, vice-principal St. Mary Hall, curate of Stanton Harcourt; admon. at Oxford 13 Nov., 1685. See Ath. iv. 479; & O.H.S. iv. 292.

Wilson, John, s. J., of Ramsbury, Wilts, cler. New Inn Hall, matric. 6 April, 1677, aged 17; perhaps vicar of Berwick St. James 1682, and Bremhill, (both) Wilts, 1687; his father John, vicar of Ramsbury 1664. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Poke, Thomas, s. Jo., of Reading, Berks, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 19 June, 1677, aged 17; B.A. from Christ Church 1682, rector of Horham 1693-1704, and of Rickinghall Superior, Suffolk, 7-22 Oct., 1712. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Richards, William, s. William, of Colbrooke, Devon, p.p. New Inn Hall, matric. 20 March, 1677-8, aged 17, B.A. 1681, M.A. 1684; perhaps fellow Eton college 1695, rector of St. Allen, Cornwall, 1709. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Davies, Edward, s. Edw., of St. Asaph, co. Flint, p.p. New Inn Hall, matric. 4 April, 1677, aged 19; B.A. 1680, vicar-choral of St. Asaph 1687, father of William 1708. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Stacey, Edmund, s. Joh., of Mere, Wilts, p.p. New Inn Hall, matric. 1 Feb., 1677-8, aged 20; B.A. from Corpus Christi Coll. 23 Feb., 1681-2; rector of Breane, Somerset, 1694. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Staverton, Richard, s. R., of Eversleigh, Hants, gent. New Inn Hall, matric. 20 Feb., 1677-8, aged 17, B.A. 1681, M.A. 1684; rector of Eversleigh 1699. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Lloyd, John, s. Hugh, of Trawsfynydd, co. Merioneth, pp. New Inn Hall, matric. 2 Nov., 1677, aged 18; one of these names rector of Trawsfynydd 1696-7. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Burton, William, s. Edw., of Kidderminster, co. Worcester, p.p. New Inn Hall, matric. 1 Feb., 1677-8, aged 17; perhaps brother of John 1665.
Bower, James, s. J., of Donat St. Andrew's, Wilts, p.p. New Inn Hall, matric. 30 March, 1677, aged 18 (called Bowyer in Mat. Reg.).
Pope, John, s. Joh., of Candlemarsh, Dorset, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 21 March, 1677-8, aged 16; father of Thomas 1707.
Carter, Walter, s. Sam., of Tavistock, Devon, p.p. New Inn Hall, matric. 8 March, 1677-8, age. 17; B.A. 12 March, 1684-5.
Boucher, Benjamin, s. Th., of Kenilworth, co. Warwick, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 21 March, 1677-8, aged 18.
North, Joshua, s. Jos., cler. New Inn Hall, matric. 19 June, 1677, aged 17; B.A. 1681, M.A. 11 March, 1683-4.
Johnson, Richard, s. William, of Kemys, co. Montgomery, pp. New Inn Hall, matric. 30 March, 1677, aged 22.
Bythell, Thomas, s. Eustace, of Clyro, co. Radnor, p.p. New Inn Hall, matric. 8 March, 1677-8, aged 18.
Gillingham, Thomas, s. William, of Odcomb, Somerset, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 11 May, 1677, aged 18.
Jones, William, s. Th., of Criccieth, co. Carmarth., pp. New Inn Hall, matric. 30 March, 1677, aged 18.
Wayte, William, s. Edw., of Fairleigh, Somerset, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 13 July, 1677, aged 17.
Apperley, John, s. William, of co. Worcester, p.p. New Inn Hall, matric. 8 March, 1677-8, aged 17.
Jones, John, s. J., of Llanyre, co. Radnor, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 19 July, 1677, aged 24.

Upham, Thomas, s. Rich., of Bickston, Devon, p.p. New Inn Hall, matric. 9 July, 1678, aged 18, B.A. 1682; rector of Combe Pyne, Devon, Dec., 1699, until his death in 1744; buried at Offwell. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Shoard, Andrew, s. Rich., of Liss, Southants, p.p. New Inn Hall, matric. 11 April, 1678, aged 18; one of these names rector of Widford 1678, and vicar of Little Baddow, Essex, 1680. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Rossington, Anthony, s. James, of St. Allen, Cornwall, paup. New Inn Hall, matric. 4 Nov., 1678, aged 22, B.A. 1682, vicar of St. Allen, 1687; brother of James 1661. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Crouch, Robert (Crowch), s. R., of Pimperne, Dorset, p.p. New Inn Hall, matric. 7 Feb., 1678-9, aged 20; vicar of South Stoke, Somerset, 1691. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Jones, Thomas, s. Th., of Llansaddwrn, co. Carmarth., pp. New Inn Hall, matric. 14 June, 1678, aged 15, B.A. 1682; perhaps vicar of Llansadwrn, 1685. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Osbaldeston, (Sir) Lacy, s. Littleton, of Woodstock, bart. New Inn Hall, matric. 10 Dec., 1678, aged 17; 2nd bart., brother of Charles, and father of William.
Jones, Daniel, s. "Joh. Ellis", of Trawsfynydd, co. Merioneth, pp. New Inn Hall, matric. 3 May, 1678, aged 19 (subs. more like "Janes").
Nicholls, Richard, s. John, of Barford, Oxon, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 28 June, 1678, aged 15; B.A. 1682, M.A. 1685.
Prichard, William, s. Richard Lewis, of Mothvey, co. Carmarthen, p.p. New Inn Hall, matric. 13 March, 1678-9, aged 22.
Williams, Thomas, s. Th., of Mothvey, co. Carmarthen, p.p. New Inn Hall, matric. 14 March, 1678-9, aged 22.
Jones, David, s. "Joh. Griffin", of Nantmel, co. Radnor, pp. New Inn Hall, matric. 25 June, 1678, aged 16.
Burges, William, s. William, of North Molton, Devon, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 24 July, 1678, aged 20.
Pringe, John, s. Joh., of Aliscombe, Devon, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 16 April, 1678, aged 22.
Butt, John, s. Robert, of Bradford, Dorset, paup. New Inn Hall, matric. 4 Nov., 1678, aged 18.

Lewis, Stephen, s. Tho., of Cregina, co. Radnor, pp. New Inn Hall, matric. 12 April, 1679, aged 24; B.A. 1682, vicar of Weobley, co. Hereford, 1690, and rector of Holgate, Salop, 1704; father of Stephen 1711, and Thomas 1704. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Roberts, Sampson, s. Sim., of Llanynys (?), co. Denbigh, paup. New Inn Hall, matric. 15 July, 1679, aged 17; B.A. 1683, M.A. 1696, rector of Llanelian 1689-1627, vicar of Bettws-yn-Rhos, co. Denbigh, 1717. See Thomas, 358; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Barton, Joseph, s. William, of Haxton, Wilts, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 28 March, 1679, aged 19; B.A. 1682, vicar of Sellinge, Kent, 1680, etc. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus. [Addenda: perhaps M.A. Lambeth, 6 July, 1708.]

Pope, Thomas, s. R., of Wimborne, Dorset, p.p. New Inn Hall, matric. 19 March, 1679-80. aged 16; B.A. 1683, M.A. 1687, vicar of Osmington 1690, and rector of Winterbourne Came, Dorset, 1704. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Perry, Thomas, s. Joh., of Wellington, Somerset, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 10 July, 1679, aged 17; B.A. 1683; one of these names vicar of St. Constantine, Cornwall, 1686. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Sutton, John, s. Tho., of Banbury, Oxon, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 19 March, 1679-80, aged 16, B.A. 1683, M.A. 1689; perhaps vicar of Loders, Dorset, 1693. See Robinson, i. 274.
Wall, Wriothesley, s. Rich., of Clavertock, Somerset, cler. New Inn Hall, matric. 1 April, 1679, aged 20; buried 26 March, 1681, in Merton Coll. chapel. See Gutch, i. 212.
Smith, John, s. Tho., of Semley, Wilts, cler. New Inn Hall., matric. 4 April, 1679, aged 17, B.A. 1682; his father rector of Semley, Wilts, 1662-1708, by Christ church.
Furneaux, John, s. J(ohn), of Stoke Damarel, pp. New Inn Hall, matric. 28 March, 1679, aged 19; B.A. 1682, curate of Plympton St. Mary, Devon, 1691; died next year.
Hext, John, s. Philip, of Swallowcliffe, Wilts, paup. New Inn Hall, matric. 16 July, 1679, aged 16; rector of Goathurst, Somerset, 1689. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Williams, Edw(ard), s. Tho., of Carmarthen (town), pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 12 March, 1679-80, aged 18; B.A. from Jesus Coll. 1683, M.A. 1686.
Squire, Richard, s. R., of Blackford, Somerset, paup. New Inn Hall, matric. 8 July, 1679, aged 18; brother of Daniel 1685, and of Samuel 1669.
Hewitt, Thomas, s. Tho., of Ladbrooke, co. Warwick, pleb, New Inn Hall, matric. 12 April, 1679, aged 14; B.A. 1682, M.A. 1685.
Coxwell, Charles, s. Jo., of Ablington, parish Bibury, co. Gloucester, gent. New Inn Hall, matric. 26 Feb., 1679-80, aged 18.
Jones, Meredith, s. John, of Machynlleth, co. Montgom., pp. New Inn Hall, matric. 28 March, 1679, aged 18; B.A. 1682.
Clymer, John, s. Richard, of Stone, co. Worcester, p.p. New Inn Hall, matric. 4 April, 1679, aged 16.
Maynard, Ezekiel, s. Joh., of Bradford, Devon, p.p. New Inn Hall, matric. 28 March, 1679, aged 19.
Corbin, William, s. 'Luc.,' of Totnes, Devon, p.p. New Inn Hall, matric. 19 Oct., 1679, aged 21.
Taylour, John, s. N., of Wimborne, Dorset, cler. New Inn Hall, matric. 5 Dec., 1679, aged 15.

Eyre, Thomas, s. Sam., of Hartlebury, co. Worcester, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 23 March, 1680-1, aged 17; B.A. from Christ Church 1684; M.A. from Oriel Coll. 1688, vicar of Staines, Middlesex, 1696; brother of Urban. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Lewis, Ellis, s. "Lewis Ellis," of Dolgelly, co. Merioneth, paup. New Inn Hall, matric. 12 March, 1680-1, aged 18; B.A. from Oriel Coll. 1685; vicar of Kilken, Flints, 1700, and rector of Rhuddlan, Flints, 1704. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Owen, Henry, s. H., of North Curry, Somerset, cler. New Inn Hall, matric. 13 July, 1680, aged 18; rector of Greinton (B.A.) 1685, and vicar of Moorlinch, Somerset, 1689, until his death in 1721. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Doidge, Richard, s. Hen., of Liskeard, Cornwall, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 26 Nov., 1680, aged 25; B.A. 1684, vicar of Talland, Cornwall, 1713, etc. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Downton, Richard, s. James, of Wedmore, Somerset, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 11 Feb., 1680-1, aged 17; B.A. 1684, vicar of Wedmore 1688-1707. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Ousley, William (Owsley), s. William, of Trull, Somerset, pp. New Inn Hall, matric. 22 Feb., 1680-1, aged 17; B.A. from All Souls' Coll. 16 March, 1684-5, M.A. 1687.
Cookesley, William, s. William, of Wellington, Somerset, paup. New Inn Hall, matric. 22 Feb., 1680-1, aged 17; B.A. 28 Feb., 1684-5, M.A. 1687.
Goldesbrough, Nicholas (Goldisbrough), s. Jon., of Mere, Wilts, paup. New Inn Hall, matric. 8 March, 1680-1, aged 20; brother of John 1676.
Bevan, Daniel, s. D., of Wallingford, Berks, paup., New Inn Hall, matric. 1 March, 1680-1, aged 16. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Buggin, John, s. J., of Shenstone, co. Worcester, paup. New Inn Hall, matric. 11 March, 1680-1, aged 16; B.A. 1684.
Butler, Caryll, s. George, of Chichester, Sussex, gent. New Inn Hall, matric. 11 Feb., 1680-1, aged 17; B.A. 1684.
Nicholls, John, s. Matth., of Burrington, Devon, cler. New Inn Hall, matric. entry 21 Feb., 1680-1, aged 13.
Bryant, Francis, s. F., of Bampton, Devon, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 11 March, 1680-1, aged 16.
Dodington, John, s. Steph., of Mere, Wilts, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 8 March, 1680-1, aged 19.

Paxton, William, s. Edm., of Bray, Berks, paup. New Inn Hall, matric. 17 Feb., 1681-2, aged 18; B.A. from Merton Coll. 25 Feb., 1685-6; M.A. from King's Coll., Cambridge, 1689, rector of Cottisford, Oxon, 1691. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Hawkins, Robert s. R, of London, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 1 June, 1681, aged 18; student of the Middle Temple 1681, as 2nd son of Robert, of Billingsgate, London, grocer. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Wintour, Charles, s. Edw., of Newland, co. Gloucester, paup. New Inn Hall, matric. 9 Dec., 1681, aged 22, B.A. 1685; perhaps of Lydney, co. Gloucester, high sheriff, and knighted 27 Aug., 1686.
Griffith, Edward, s. E., of Mold, co. Flint, paup. New Inn Hall, matric. 4 June, 1681, aged 18; B.A. 1685, M.A. 1688.
Whitford, Thomas, s. Joh., of St. Columb Minor, Cornwall, paup. New Inn Hall, matric. 3 March, 1681-2, aged 18.

Jervoise, Thomas, s. Th., of Herriard, Hants, arm. New Inn Hall, matric. 2 March, 1682-3, aged 15; of Herriard, M.P. Stockbridge (May), 1691-5, Hants 1698-1702, Plympton, 1702, till unseated Jan., 1703, Hindon (Nov.), 1704-April, 1705, Hants 1705-8, and Dec., 1709-Sept., 1710; died 10 May, 1743, aged 79; brother of Richard. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.
Evans, Evan, s. John, of Llangoedmore, co. Cardigan, pp. New Inn Hall, matric. 8 Dec., 1682, aged 18; B.A. 1686, perhaps vicar of Clydey, co. Pembroke, 1688. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Butt, Thomas, s. Rob., of Bradford, Dorset, paup. New Inn Hall, matric. 30 May, 1682, aged 17; B.A. 12 March, 1685-6, vicar of Mere, Wilts, 1693. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Chelmicke, John, s. Rob., of Kingsland, co. Hereford, p.p. New Inn Hall, matric. 17 Oct., 1682, aged 16; vicar of Vowchurch, co. Hereford, 1690. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Taylor, Francis, s. Jerem., of Shobden, co. Hereford, paup. New Inn Hall, matric. 17 Oct., 1682, aged 19, B.A. 1686; vicar of Winscombe, Somerset, 1697. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Harwood, Thomas s. John, of New Windsor, Berks, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 21 July, 1682, aged 17; B.A. 1686; if brother of John 1679; then seated at Tern, Salop.
Herward, Thomas, s. Nic., of Ludgershall, Bucks, cler. New Inn Hall, matric. 6 July, 1682, aged 15; his father rector 1662-83. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Carew, Edward, s. John, of Harberton, Devon, cler. New Inn Hall, matric. 23 March, 1682-3, aged 19. See Matthew 1668, brother of Thomas 1685.
Bunning, David, s. Tho., of Stony Stanton, co. Leicester, cler. New Inn Hall, matric. 6 July, 1682, aged 19; B.A. 1686.
Griffyth, Owen, s. "Griffith Owens", of Permorva, co. "Carmar," pp. New Inn Hall, matric. 15 July, 1682, aged 19.
Condy, Francis, s. Edm., of Lamerton, Devon, cler. New Inn Hall, matric. 23 March, 1682-3, aged 17; B.A. 1686.
Cary, John, s. Nathaniel, of Yeovil, Somerset, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 15 July, 1682, aged 16; B.A. 1686.
Dabbs, Richard, s. Tho., of Netherstow, Somerset, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 22 March, 1682-3, aged 28.
Browne, Thomas, s. Th., of Ensham, Oxon, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 7 March, 1683-4, aged 17; B.A. 1687, M.A. 1690, one of these names vicar of Shiplake, Oxon, 1700. See Foster's Index Ecc.
Whittney, Eustace, s. Eustace, of Boughrood, co. Radnor, gent. New Inn Hall, matric. 19 Feb., 1683-4, aged 21; vicar of Clifford, co. Hereford, 1687. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Sheppard, Thomas, s. Th., of London, paup. New Inn Hall, matric. 6 Dec., 1683, aged 14, B.A. 1687, M.A. 1690.
King, Francis, of New Inn Hall, pleb., matric. 15 May, 1683.
Smith, John, cler. fil. New Inn Hall, matric. 26 June, 1683.
Bisse, Francis, New Inn Hall, matric. 23 May, 1683.

Hibbert, Thomas (Hebbert), s. Th., of Sarum, Wilts, paup. New Inn Hall, matric. 22 July, 1684, aged 20; B.A. 1688, vicar of Ellingham, Hants, 1695, rector of East Stoke, Dorset, 1696. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Jenkins, David, s. Tho., of Llandigwy, co. Cardigan, paup. New Inn Hall, matric. 13 May, 1684, aged 18; B.A. 1689, M.A. 1694; perhaps vicar of Cardigan 1692; see also next man. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Shepeard, Samuel, s. William, of Preston, Northants, paup. New Inn Hall, matric. 29 April, 1684, aged 16, B.A. 17 Jan., 1687-8; perhaps vicar of Milton Ernest, Beds, 1721-3. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Hole, Joshua, s. Nic., of Broadwood Kelly, Devon, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 13 May, 1684, aged 18; B.A. 2 March, 1687-8, as Joseph.

Mason, George, s. Robert, of Fruxon, co. Hereford, cler. New Inn Hall, matric. 10 Oct., 1685, aged 17; B.A. 1689, M.A. 1693; rector of Kington, co. Worcester, 1694; his father perhaps rector of Whitchurch St. Dubritius, co. Hereford, 1669. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Petipher, John, s. Richard, of Blakesley, Northants, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 10 April, 1685, aged 18, B.A. 1688; vicar of Blakesley 1692, until his death; buried there 7 Nov., 1729; father of the next. See Baker's Northants, ii. 25; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Guidott, William, s. William, of Wotton, Hants, gent. New Inn Hall, matric. 22 March, 1685-6, aged 14: student of Lincoln's Inn 1686, M.P. Andover in seven parliaments, 1708-27, and (Jan.) 1730-41; died 30 Aug., 1745. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Parre, Robert, s. R., of Kirkby, co. Leicester, cler. New Inn Hall, matric. 27 March, 1685, aged 17; B.A. 1688, M.A. 1691, incorporated at Cambridge 1697, rector of Willey, co. Warwick, 1701; brother of Samuel 1678. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Carew, Thomas, s. Joh., of Harberton, Devon, paup. New Inn Hall, matric. 10 Nov., 1685, aged 18; B.A. 1689, one of these names vicar of Porchester, Hants, 1693, brother of Edward 1683. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Jervoise, Richard, s. Tho., of Herriard, Hants, gent. New Inn Hall, matric. 22 March, 1685-6, aged 16; bar.-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1700; brother of Thomas 1683. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Cranford, Nathaniel, s. Joh., of Elsfield, Hants, cler. New Inn Hall, matric. 22 March, 1685-6, aged 17; B.A. 1689, perhaps son of the last named Nathaniel rather than of a John.
Sleigh, William, s. Hug., of Drainfield, Dorset, paup. New Inn Hall, matric. 19 Feb., 1685-6, aged 23.
Edwards, William, s. William, of Gloubery, Salop, paup. New Inn Hall, matric. 3 April, 1685, aged 18.
Davies, Isaac, s. Lewis, of Bettus, Salop, paup. New Inn Hall, matric. 2 June, 1685, aged 23.

Sloper, William, s. Tho., of Hartpury, co. Gloucester, paup. New Inn Hall, matric. 17 Dec., 1686, aged 17; of the Inner Temple 1707, perhaps M.P. Bedwyn, 1715, Camelford 1722, Bedwyn (March), 1729-41, Whitchurch Jan., 1742, until his death 14 Jan., 1743, father of the next-named. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.

Nutley, William, s. William, of London, gent. New Inn Hall, matric. 29 Oct., 1686, aged 16; bar.-at-law, Middle Temple, 1693; licenced (V.G.) 9 March, 1691-2, to marry Amy Hooper, of St. Paul's, Covent Garden, Middlesex, spinster. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Coxhead, Thomas, s. Th., of Oxford (city), paup. New Inn Hall, matric. 26 March, 1686, aged 17; B.A. from St. Alban Hall 20 Feb., 1689-90, M.A. from Magdalen Hall 14 Jan., 1717-18, vicar of Thundridge, Herts, 1696. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Jackman, John, s. Francis, of Haslemere, Surrey, pp. New Inn Hall, matric. 26 March, 1686, aged 14; B.A. 1689, M.A. 1692, vicar of Leeke Wootton, co. Warwick, 1701, and of Long Itchington 1710. See Rawl. ii. 188; & Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Evans, Hugh, s. Evan, of Llanvihangel, co. Cardigan, paup. New Inn Hall, matric. 5 July, 1686, aged 18; B.A. from Corpus Christi Coll. 1690, incorporated at Cambridge 1720.
Sa(u)nders, John, s. William, of St. Bartholomew's, Oxon, paup. New Inn Hall, matric. 20 May, 1686, aged 17; B.A. from Queen's Coll. 1690, M.A. 1693.
Dewey, John, s. J., of Andover, Hants, paup. New Inn Hall, matric. 16 March, 1686-7, aged 16; B.A. 1 March, 1691-2, called Dervey in Mat. Reg.
Canne, Jasper, s. 'G.' (? 'Gasparus'), of Witney, Oxon, cler. New Inn Hall, matric. 11 March, 1686-7, aged 18; brother of Thomas same date.
Swayne, Abraham (Swaine), s. Joh., of "Graisly Templ," co. Derby, gent. New Inn Hall, matric. 20 May, 1686, aged 18, B.A. 1690, M.A. 1693.
Harris, Richard, s. Rich., of Clungun(ford), Salop, gent. New Inn Hall, matric. 26 March, 1686, aged 20.
Pratt, Wyatt, s. Rich., of Cullompton, Devon, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 17 Dec., 1687, aged 17; B.A. 1691, rector of Norton Fitzwarren, Somerset, 1697. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Philips, Stephen, s. S(tephen), of Hereford (city), paup. New Inn Hall, matric. 3 July, 1688, aged 17; B.A. from Brasenose Coll. 1692, student of Inner Temple 1692 (S.T.D. fil.), vicar of Beddingham, Sussex, 1696-1701, and vicar of Bisley, co. Gloucester, 1715; brother of Robert 1693. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg. & Foster's Index Eccl.

Limbrey, John, s. Henr., of Hodington, Hants, gent. New Inn Hall, matric. 28 Feb., 1688-9, aged 17; student of Gray's Inn 1690 (his father of London, merchant); licenced 15 July, 1696, to marry Martha, daughter of Sir John Huband, bart. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg. & London Marriage Licences, ed. Foster.

Cobden, William, s. William, of Elsfield, co. Southants, cler. New Inn Hall, matric. 28 March, 1688, aged 17, B.A. 1691; M.A. from Lincoln Coll. 1694, rector of Lurgashall, Sussex, 1698-1723, brother of Richard 1695. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Quick, John, s. Alexander, of Wrenton, Somerset, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 28 March, 1688, aged 17; B.A. 22 Feb., 1691-2, M.A. 1694, rector of Little Tinterne, co. Monmouth, 1697, and of Highbray, Devon, 1706. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Malcher, John, s. J., of Wootton, Oxon, gent. New Inn Hall, matric. 7 April, 1688, aged 15; student of Inner Temple 1691, as son and heir of John, of Clifford's Inn, gent., deceased. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Jones, Howell, s. H., of Llangoedmore, co. Cardig., paup. New Inn Hall, matric. 3 July, 1688, aged 18; B.A. from Trinity Coll. 1692; rector of Nash and Upton, co. Pembroke, 1695. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Hol(l)oway, Charles, s. William, of Marsham, Berks, paup. New Inn Hall, matric. 26 June, 1688, aged 18; B.A. 13 March, 1699-1700, vicar of Rodmersham, Kent, 1713. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Nutley, Richard, s. William, of London, gent. New Inn Hall, matric. 28 March, 1688, aged 15; B.A. 1691, M.A. 1694, bar.-at-law, Middle Temple, 1698. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Morris, James, s. Tho., of Cardigan (town), paup. New Inn Hall, matric. 28 Feb, 1688-9, aged 19.
Balster, Jacob, of Wincanton, Somerset, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 7 April, 1688, aged 19.

Millichampe, Richard, s. Tim., of Ludlow, Salop, cler. New Inn Hall, matric. 24 March, 1689-90, aged 18, B.A. 7 March, 1694-5; incorporated at King's Coll., Cambridge, 1702, M.A. 1702, rector of Rudford, co. Gloucester, 1697, vicar of Great Marlow, Bucks, 1708, rector of Radford, co. Gloucester, 1708, until his death in 1729. See Rawl. iii. 250; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Pollen, Edward, s. Joh., of Andover, Hants, arm. New Inn Hall, matric. 1 June, 1689, aged 17; born 30 May, 1672, student of Lincoln's Inn 1692; died 1731; father of John 1714. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Hill, Anthony, s. A(nthony), of Steeple Gidding, Huntingdon, cler. New Inn Hall, matric. 11 Oct., 1689, aged 18; chaplain to the Duke of Richmond, vicar of Boldre, Hants, 1703. See Rawl. iv. 339.
Harris, John (Harrys), s. "Henr. Jones", of Mydrim, co. Carmarthen, paup. New Inn Hall, matric. 16 May, 1689, aged 19; perhaps B.C.L. 26 Nov., 1715.
Price, John, of Glascombe, co. Radnor, paup. New Inn Hall, matric. 16 May, 1689, aged 17; B.A. from Merton Coll. 16 March, 1692-3.
James, John, s. Ja., of Llansawell, co. Caer., pp. New Inn Hall, matric. 12 April, 1690, aged 18; B.A. from Jesus Coll. 17 Feb., 1693-4.
Williams, John, s. Jo., of Llanboydy, co. Carnarvon, p.p. New Inn Hall, matric. 31 March, 1690, aged 18; B.A. from St. Mary Hall 1694.
Richards, Edward, s. Edw., of Southampton (town), gent. New Inn Hall, matric. 11 April, 1690, aged 18; B.A. 1693. See Edward 1660.
Robinson, Walter, s. Walter, of Andover, Hants, gent. New Inn Hall, matric. 11 April, 1690, aged 16; brother of William 1679.
Williams, David, paup. New Inn Hall, matric. 12 March, 1690-1.

Braman, Nicholas, s. Th., of Alton, Hants, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 17 March, 1691-2, aged 16; B.A. from Magdalen Hall 1695, M.A. 1698, chaplain of Wadham College 1698-1711, rector of Byfleet, Surrey, 1711. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus & Gardiner, 397.
Penton, James, s. Edw., of Bruntingthorpe, co. Leicester, cler. New Inn Hall, matric. 19 Nov., 1691, aged 20; B.A. 1695, vicar of Great Peatling, co. Leicester, 1701. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Franke, Abraham, s. A., of Exborne, Devon, cler. New Inn Hall, matric. 31 March, 1691, aged 19; B.A. 1694, vicar of Burrington, Devon, 1696-1732. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Mordaunt, George, born in London, s. John, Viscount Mordaunt. New Inn Hall. matric. 27 Jan., 1691-2, aged 15; in orders; died 28 July, 1728; brother of Charles 1674, etc.
Hayter, George, s. G(eorge), of Chagford, Devon, cler. New Inn Hall, matric. 18 March, 1691-2, aged 18; B.A. 1698, rector of Chagford 1702. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Mott, John, s. Nath., of Lapley, co. Stafford, cler. New Inn Hall, matric. 19 June, 1691, aged 17; vicar of Lapley 1700. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Stokes, Edward, s. E(dward), of Newton, co. Gloucester, gent. New Inn Hall, matric. 19 Nov., 1691, aged 26; born 25 Dec., 1663.
Knapton, Ferdinand, s. F., of Southampton, gent. New Inn Hall, matric. 4 July, 1691, aged 19.
Godfrey, William, s. Ri., of Southampton, gent. New Inn Hall, matric. 4 July, 1691, aged 16.
Saunders, John, s. Joh., of Mongewell, Oxon, arm. New Inn Hall, matric. 16 March, 1692-3, aged 15.
Wither, William, s. Tho., of South Hampton, Middlesex, gent. New Inn Hall, matric. 13 March, 1693-4, aged 17, student of Lincoln's Inn 1694. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Silke, Thomas, s. Tho., of Chew Magna, Somerset, p.p. New Inn Hall, matric. 26 May, 1693, aged 18.
Jones, Henry, s. Henry, cler. New Inn Hall, matric. 1 June, 1693, aged 17.

Wightwick, Thomas, s. John, of Wolverhampton, co. Stafford, cler. New Inn Hall, matric. 17 March, 1694-5, aged 17; B.A. from St. Edmund Hall 20 Feb., 1699-1700; vicar of Cosby 1710, and of Shackerstone, (both) co. Leicester, 1714. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Stopes, James, s. James, of Britwell, Oxon, cler. New Inn Hall, matric. 16 March, 1694-5, aged 18, B.A. 1698, M.A. 1701; vicar of South Stoke 1701, and of Britwell Salome, Oxon, 1706. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Rudyerd, John, arm. New Inn Hall, matric. 4 March, 1694-5; student of Gray's Inn 1696, as son and heir of Benjamin, of Winchfield, Southants. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Holland, John, s. Ric., of Barton, co. Stafford, pp. New Inn Hall, matric. 17 March, 1694-5, aged 18; B.A. 1698.

Hughes, Roger, s. Hug., of Hirnant, co. Montgomery, pp. New Inn Hall, matric. 8 Feb., 1696-7, aged 20; B.A. from Christ Church 1701; one of these names beneficed in Warwickshire. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Lloyd, William, s. Rog., of Llanervil, co. Montgomery, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 8 Feb., 1696-7, aged 20.
Davers, George, s. G., of Winscombe, Somerset, cler. New Inn Hall, matric. 25 Oct., 1698, aged 15 his father's name does not appear among the institutions.
Nickolson, John, s. Tho., of Emby, "North," (? Embleton, Northumberland), pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 1 July, 1698, aged 17 (subs. paup.).

Monck, Gorstelowe, s. Tho., of Gretton, Northants, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 23 March, 1699-1700, aged 17; B.A. from Balliol Coll. 1703, M.A. 1707, incorporated at Cambridge 1708, rector of Hamstall Ridware, co. Stafford, 1715. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Musgrave, George, s. G(eorge), of Nettlecombe, Somerset, arm. New Inn Hall, matric. 18 Nov., 1699, aged 16; student of Middle Temple 1698, a colonel of militia; died 8 Sept., 1724, aged 42. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Johnson, Edward, s. E., of Ewelme, Oxon, cler. New Inn Hall, matric. 31 March, 1699, aged 17; B.A. 1 March 1702-3; perhaps vicar of East Pennard, Somerset, 1713, canon of Wells 1720. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Williams, Athelstan, s. A., of Llananno, co. Radnor, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 4 May, 1699, aged 16; vicar of Glascombe, co. Radnor, 1722. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Ellis, Thomas, s. Ellis, of Llangenog, co. Montgomery, pp. New Inn Hall, matric. 22 Dec., 1699, aged 23.
Footman, Jeremy, s. James, of Llangunlo, co. Radnor, pp. New Inn Hall, matric. 4 May, 1699, aged 19.
Theed, Richard, s. Joh., of Horton Hall (?), Bucks (?), gent. New Inn Hall, matric. 12 July, 1700, aged 16, B.A. 26 Feb., 1703-4; M.A. from Hart Hall 1706. See Rawl. i. 329.
Cooke, Morgan, s. M(organ), of Trentishoe, Devon, cler. New Inn Hall, matric. 7 June, 1700, aged 17; rector of Trentishoe, Devon, 1724. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Mosse, John, s. Rob., of Stafford (town), cler. New Inn Hall, matric. 9 Dec., 1700, aged 19.
Pulford, Joshua, s. William, of Alston, Salop, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 3 Nov., 1702, aged 19, B.A. 1706; vicar of Leighton Buzzard, Beds, 1710. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Dolman, John, s. Nathaniel, of Aldridge, co. Stafford, cler. New Inn Hall, matric. 1 June, 1703, aged 16; B.A. 6 March, 1706-7, M.A. 1710, incorp. at Cambridge 1716, rector of Aldridge 1718, canon of Lichfield 1721, brother of Thomas 1702. See Le Neve, i. 598; & Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Loxdale, Nicholas, s. Nicholas, of Boningale, Salop, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 1 June, 1703, aged 18; B.A. 17 March, 1706-7; M.A. from Jesus Coll., Cambridge, 1713, vicar of Seighford 1723, and of Ronton, co. Stafford, 1727. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Thomas, Acton, s. Acton, of Tenbury, co. Worcester, p.p. New Inn Hall, matric. 13 April, 1703, aged 18, B.A. 6 March, 1706-7; rector of Collington, co. Hereford, 1715. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Herring, John, s. John, of Lambourne, Berks, gent. New Inn Hall, matric. 16 Nov., 1703, aged 17; student of the Inner Temple 1704. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Allestree, James, s. Tho., of Wolverhampton, co. Stafford, cler. New Inn Hall, matric. 6 April, 1704, aged 18; B.A. 1707, M.A. 1710.
Griffin, James, s. William, of Fenny Compton, co. Warwick, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 7 March, 1705-6, aged 18; B.A. 1709, M.A. 9 March, 1713-14.
Rock, John, s. J., pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 28 March, 1705, aged 18; B.A. 1708.

Jackson, Humphrey, s. H., of Mevison, co. Stafford, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 12 Dec., 1706, aged 16; B.A. from Trinity Coll. 1710; M.A. from Jesus Coll., Cambridge, 1714, rector of Willesley 1721, served the cure of Measham 1734, vicar of Stapenhill (all co. Derby) 1736 until his death 11 Dec., 1757. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Jones, Samuel, s. And., of Radford, co. Warwick, cler. New Inn Hall, matric. 18 March, 1707-8, aged 17; B.A. 1711, M.A. 1714; perhaps vicar of Atcham, Salop, 1711. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Ottley, John (Oatley), s. J., of Worfield, Salop, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 7 Nov., 1707, aged 20; B.A. 1711, as Oakely.
Owens, Owen, s. O., of Beriew, co. Montgomery, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 18 March, 1707-8, aged 18.
Crowther, Edmund, s. E., of Claverly, Salop, p.p. New Inn Hall, matric. 30 March, 1707, aged 17.

Silke, James, s. Thomas, of Cullompton, Devon, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 17 May, 1708, aged 21; B.A. from All Souls' Coll. 26 Feb., 1711-12; M.A. from Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge, 1730; vicar of Bridgerule 1713, rector of Buckland Filleigh 1717, and of North Lew (all Devon) 1737. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Cuthbert, Robert, s. George, of Hendred, Berks, cler. New Inn Hall, matric. 27 March, 1708, aged 17; B.A. 1711, his father instituted to the rectory of East Hendred 17 April, 1684(-1710).

Whittell, Thomas, s. William, of Aymstrey (4to.), co. Hereford, cler. New Inn Hall, matric. 12 July, 1709, aged 20; vicar of Yarpole, co. Hereford, 1718; brother of William 1699. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Hayne, John, s. Samuel, of "Heylumister" (Ilminster), Somerset, gent. New Inn Hall, matric. 8 Feb., 1709-10, aged 18.
Prichard, John, s. Evan, of Llangadfan, co. Montgomery, cler. New Inn Hall, matric. 6 March, 1709-10, aged 19.
Mellor, Thomas, s. Tho., of Goathurst, Bucks, cler. New Inn Hall, matric. 5 Dec., 1709, aged 17; B.A. 1713.
Dewe, Henry, s. Bennett, of Hanney, Bucks, gent. New Inn Hall, matric. 3 June, 1709, aged 16.

Llewelin, Simon, s. Francis, of Cowbridge, co. Glam., cler. New Inn Hall, matric. 25 May, 1710, aged 17, B.A. 18 Feb., 1713-14; M.A. from Pembroke Coll., Cambridge, 1725, vicar of Cuthbert or Cubert 1732, and of St. Minver, Cornwall, 1735; brother of Francis 1711. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Brabourne, John, s. John, of Charlbury, Oxon, doctoris. New Inn Hall, matric. 6 March, 1710-11, aged 14; B.A. 1714, M.A. 1717, vice-principal 1717, a proctor in the Vice-Chancellor's Court, died 21 June, 1729. See Bloxam, vi. 38.
Dobinson, Richard, s. William, of Thame, Oxon, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 5 March, 1710-11, aged 16; B.A. 1714, M.A. 1717, vicar of Fotheringay, Northants, 1736. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Fernihough, Thomas, s. John, of Dilhorn, co. Stafford, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 23 Feb., 1710-11, aged 19; B.A. 1714.
Pearson, Thomas, s. Sam., of Shrewsbury, cler. New Inn Hall, matric. 25 May, 1710, aged 18.

Gage, Thomas, s. T., of Southhurst, Somerset, gent. New Inn Hall, matric. 29 Oct., 1711, aged 18; B.A. 1715, M.A. 1718, vicar of Bisham, Berks, 1723, of Little Marlow, Bucks, 1725, until his death 7 Feb., 1763. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Webber, John, s. Jonat, of St. K(ew), Cornwall, gent. New Inn Hall, matric. 8 May, 1711, aged 17; B.A. from Exeter Coll. 22 Feb., 1714-15; vicar of St. Eval, Cornwall, 1721. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Sommers, John, s. Jos., of Shipton, co. Gloucester, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 23 Nov., 1711, aged 20; perhaps rector of Nympsfield, co. Gloucester, 1720. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Llewelin, Francis, s. F., of St. Minver, Cornwall, cler. New Inn Hall, matric. 8 May, 1711, aged 16; B.A. from All Souls' Coll. 22 Feb., 1714-15; brother of Simon.

Whitfield, Henry, s. Hen., of Bradwell, Oxon, cler. New Inn Hall, matric. 19 July, 1712, aged 16; B.A. from All Souls' Coll. 1716; M.A. from Hart Hall 1719; vicar of Bradwell, Oxon, 1727, and rector of Castle Eaton, Wilts, 1742. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Stanton, Edward (Staunton), s. Sandys, of Charlbury, Oxon, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 13 Feb., 1712-13, aged 16, B.A. 1716, as Stanton.
Vaughan, Hugh, gent., of New Inn Hall, matric. 20 March, 1712-13.
Salter, James, s. James, of St. Mary Church, Devon, cler. New Inn Hall, matric. 29 April, 1713, aged 17, B.A. 1717; vicar of St. Mary Church 1718, until his death in 1767. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Boughton, John, s. Edw., of Cleobury Mortimer, Salop, cler. New Inn Hall, matric. 28 March, 1713, aged 18; B.A. 1 March, 1716-17.
Webb, Thomas, s. John, of Bruton, Somerset, cler. New Inn Hall, matric. 28 March, 1713, aged 16; B.A. from Hart Hall 1717.
Kebby, Michael, s. Benjamin, of Staplegrove, Somerset, cler. New Inn Hall, matric. 18 May, 1713, aged 17; B.A. 1717.
Annis, Benjamin, s. Ben., of Avon Dassett, co. Warwick. New Inn Hall, matric. 14 July, 1713, aged 20.
Boswell, Andrew, s. A., of Elsmore, Salop, pleb. New Inn Hall, matric. 28 March, 1713, aged 18.
Chamberlain, Nicholas, s. Jos., of White Ash, co. Warwick, paup. New Inn Hall, matric. 1 Dec., 1714, aged 18.
